sectionsUnavailable: Authenticated Sequencing.com sample report rows are not available yet.
Exact caller version, command line, callable-region mask, filter thresholds, ploidy handling, phasing, and pipeline checksum are not supplied.
Benchmark truth-set comparison, ClinVar per-record assertions, VEP transcript consequences, structural-variant support, and copy-number support are not supplied.
reportOverview.plainEnglishScope: This report summarizes selected derived candidate variant-discovery metrics. It does not confirm variant truth, diagnose disease, estimate risk, or recommend medical action.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The derived small-variant summary reports 3,820,000 candidate SNV records and 612,000 candidate indel records after upstream alignment, duplicate marking, and read-quality filtering. The exact caller version and command line are unavailable.
resultRows.sourceLabel: GATK Germline short variant discovery; GATK HaplotypeCaller; BCFtools variant calling
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: These counts describe a candidate small-variant discovery output. They are not by themselves proof that every candidate record is correct or medically meaningful.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The derived filter summary reports 3,710,000 candidate records passing the supplied filter label, 722,000 candidate records below at least one supplied quality label, and 14,200 candidate records flagged for low depth or genotype-quality context. Exact thresholds are unavailable.
resultRows.sourceLabel: SAMtools mpileup; BCFtools variant calling; GATK HaplotypeCaller
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Filter labels help explain which candidate records had stronger or weaker supporting evidence, but they cannot replace full pipeline settings, validation, or expert review.

resultRows.item: dbSNP identifier availability and unavailable annotation rows
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The derived cross-reference summary reports dbSNP identifiers for 91.6% of candidate records. ClinVar lookup status, submitted clinical assertions, VEP transcript consequences, and Sequence Ontology terms are not supplied for individual records.
resultRows.sourceLabel: NCBI dbSNP; NCBI ClinVar; Ensembl VEP; Sequence Ontology
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: A public identifier can help look up a candidate record, but database presence does not make the record clinically important and does not provide personal medical meaning.

resultRows.item: Benchmark, callable-region, and pipeline metadata unavailable
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: Benchmark truth-set comparison, callable-region mask, per-chromosome failure table, ploidy handling, phasing, structural-variant support, copy-number support, caller command line, pipeline checksum, and authenticated sample-report rows are unavailable.
resultRows.sourceLabel: NIST Genome in a Bottle; GATK Germline short variant discovery
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Without benchmark and pipeline metadata, the report can explain supplied discovery metrics but cannot validate analytical performance, clinical sensitivity, or per-record correctness.

variantDiscoveryContext.plainEnglishMeaning: Variant discovery is the process of identifying candidate sequence differences from a sequencing workflow. The supplied metrics describe that workflow output at a summary level, not clinical meaning for any specific candidate record.
unsupportedClaims: observed variant truth or variant absence
clinical sensitivity or negative predictive value
ancestry certainty or exact population assignment
screening, diagnostic testing, reproductive, lifestyle, or treatment recommendation
variantDiscoveryContext.professionalReview: Laboratory or clinical review is needed to interpret candidate variant records against a specific assay, caller configuration, validation study, and reporting threshold.

genotypeSummary.observedValue: 3,820,000 candidate SNV records and 612,000 candidate indel records; exact caller version and command line unavailable
3,710,000 candidate records passing supplied filter label; 722,000 below at least one supplied quality label; 14,200 flagged for low depth or genotype-quality context
dbSNP identifiers available for 91.6% of candidate records; individual ClinVar, VEP, and Sequence Ontology annotations unavailable
benchmark, callable-region, per-chromosome failure, ploidy, phasing, structural-variant, copy-number, caller command, pipeline checksum, and authenticated sample-report metadata unavailable

resourceVersions: GATK germline short variant discovery documentation accessed 2026-06-20
missingInputs: Exact caller version, command line, and pipeline checksum
Callable-region mask and per-chromosome failure table
Ploidy, phasing, structural-variant, and copy-number support
Per-record ClinVar assertions, VEP transcript consequences, and Sequence Ontology terms
uncertainty: No calibrated model is supplied for variant correctness probability, clinical sensitivity, disease probability, pathogenicity probability, or overall discovery accuracy probability.
The selected metrics are useful QC context but cannot validate a clinical report or prove per-record correctness by themselves.

calibration.notes: Benchmark truth-set comparison, per-record concordance, and calibrated quality model details were not supplied.

limitations: This validation result uses selected derived variant-discovery metrics only; raw genome data, VCF, FASTQ, BAM, and CRAM files are not included.
Candidate variant records are tool- and filter-dependent and should not be treated as confirmed variant truth without supporting validation evidence.

The package does not diagnose disease, classify pathogenicity, estimate medical risk, validate clinical sensitivity, infer ancestry certainty, or recommend medication, screening, diagnostic testing, reproductive decisions, lifestyle changes, or treatment changes.
